waterbody_name: FRASER RIVER (upstream of the CPR Bridge at Mission)
raw_text:
Dead fin fish may be used for bait only when fishing for sturgeon (see page 8 in the Provincial Regulations)
**No Fishing** in the area bounded by a line commencing at a fishing boundary sign located at the eastern end of Landstrom Bar (Scale Bar)
to a fishing boundary sign on the opposite bank of the river, thence to a fishing boundary sign at the southern end of Croft Island, thence
to a fishing boundary sign on the nearest bank of the river in a westerly direction, thence following the bank of the river in a westerly
direction to the Point of Commencement, May 1-Oct 31
**No Fishing** in the non-tidal portion of the Fraser River in Region 2 from one hour after sunset to one hour before sunrise
**No Fishing** in the Jesperson's Side Channel, Herrling Side Channel, and Seabird Island north Side Channel May 15-July 31. See page 26
for a map of the No Fishing areas
**No Fishing** for steelhead Fraser River Mainstem Aug 15-Dec 31
Boats less than 20 m in length, under power or anchored, must not impede the passage of commercial vessels

raw_text:
Because regional quotas and gear restrictions are different in lakes and streams, please note: the channel between Lillooet Lake and
Little Lillooet Lake is part of the Lillooet River. This channel starts at the southern end of Lillooet Lake where it is defined by the point of
land on the west side and by a sign immediately across the channel and next to the main logging road on the east. Little Lillooet Lake's
lower boundary is approximately 1 km upstream of Billygoat Creek (see map below and Little Lillooet Lake's entry below)
cleaned_text: Because regional quotas and gear restrictions are different in lakes and streams, please note: the channel between Lillooet Lake and Little Lillooet Lake is part of the Lillooet River. Little Lillooet Lake's lower boundary is approximately 1 km upstream of Billygoat Creek (see map below and Little Lillooet Lake's entry below).

raw_text:
Exempt from spring closure upstream of Thompson River, except as noted below
**No Fishing **for trout in Fraser River mainstem: downstream of Hell's Gate Sept 1-Nov 15, from Hell's Gate to the confluence with the
Thompson River from Oct 1-Nov 30, and upstream of the confluence with the Thompson River Oct 1-Oct 31
**No Fishing** between fishing boundary signs approximately 6.5 km downstream of Boston Bar to signs 2.8 km downstream of
Hell's Gate
**No Fishing** from the lower entrance of the fish ladder at Bridge River Rapids to the BC Railway Bridge approximately 2 km north of
Lillooet, Mar 31-June 20
From Hwy 99 bridge at Lillooet downstream approximately 1.2 km to BC Hydro's tail race outflow channel: **No Fishing** Oct 1-May 31;
From the confluence with Thompson River to the CNR Bridge approximately 1 km downstream: **No Fishing**, Oct 1-May 31; bait ban and no
hooks greater than 15 mm from point to shank, Oct 1-Dec 31
**No Fishing** for steelhead from the confluence with Spuzzum Creek (Region 3 boundary) to Hells Gate Aug 15-Dec 31
**No Fishing** for steelhead from Hells Gate upstream to the Region 3 boundary Jan 1-Dec 31

waterbody_name: SHUSWAP LAKE (see maps on page 28) (includes Little Shuswap Lake, that part of South Thompson River between Shuswap Lake and Little Shuswap Lake, Seymour, Anstey and Salmon arms and Mara Lake unless stated otherwise)
raw_text:
**No Fishing** Mar 15-May 14, and **rainbow trout and char catch and release**, May 15-Mar 14, in the waters lying west of a line between
signs at Henstridge Road and Wharf Road to a line between signs on the south and north shores of Little Shuswap Lake (see map A on
page 28)
**No Fishing** in the entire area north of Albas, Mar 15-May 14 (see map B on page 28)
**No Fishing** east of a line between fishing boundary signs on Murdock and Semaphore points, to Hwy 1 bridge, Mar 15-May 14 (see map C
on page 28)
**No fishing** in Salmon Arm Bay, west of line between Engineer's Point and Sunnybrae Point (see map on page 28), for the conservation
of chinook and coho salmon stocks, Sept 1-Dec 31 (anglers fishing from the community pier in the city of Salmon Arm are exempt from
the closure)
**No Ice Fishing**
Rainbow trout daily quota = 1 (none under 50 cm), annual quota = 5
Char daily quota = 1 (none under 60 cm), annual quota = 5
Bait ban, single barbless hook (anglers fishing from the community pier in the city of Salmon Arm are exempt from the bait ban)
Conservation Surcharge Stamp required to catch and keep rainbow trout over 50 cm or char over 60 cm

raw_text:
**No Fishing** upstream of Iltasyuko River[Includes Tributaries], including upstream of Anahim Lake[Includes Tributaries], Apr 1-June 14
**No Fishing** from Iltasyuko River to Crag Creek[Includes Tributaries]
**No Fishing** from Crag Creek to fishing boundary signs approximately 500 m upstream of canyon[Includes Tributaries], Oct 1-May 31
**No Fishing** from fishing boundary signs approximately 500 m upstream of canyon to signs 100 m downstream of canyon
**No Fishing** from fishing boundary signs approximately 100 m downstream of canyon to tidal boundary, Oct 1 - May 31
Trout/char daily quota = 1 (none under 35 cm)
Bait ban all parts[Includes Tributaries]
Fly fishing only from** (a)** Anahim Lake to Iltasyuko River, June 15- Mar 31, and** (b)** Crag Creek to fishing boundary signs approximately 500 m
upstream of canyon, July 15-Sept 30
No powered boats between signs 0.5 km and 3.5 km upstream of canyon, Aug 1-Aug 31
**From Crag Creek to signs 500 m upstream of the canyon**[Includes Tributaries]**: Class I water June 1-Sept 30 and Steelhead Stamp mandatory June 1-Sept 30; see map below (Non-residents see notice on **page 42**)
From signs 100 m downstream of canyon to tidal boundary**[Includes Tributaries]**: Class I water June 1-Sept 30 and Steelhead Stamp mandatory June 1-Sept 30: see map below
From Anahim Lake to Iltasyuko River**[Includes Tributaries]**: Class II water June 16-Oct 31 (see map below); Steelhead Stamp not required**
NOTE: "canyon" means lower canyon 3-5 km from river's mouth

From signs 100 m downstream of canyon to tidal boundary [Includes Tributaries]: Class I water June 1-Sept 30 and Steelhead Stamp mandatory June 1-Sept 30; see map below. Lake trout daily and possession quotas = 2 (only 1 over 90 cm, none between 60 cm and 90 cm); Arctic grayling daily and possession
quotas = 4 (only 1 over 44 cm, none between 36 cm and 44 cm); northern pike daily and possession quotas = 4 (only 1 over 100 cm, none
between 70 cm and 100 cm); whitefish daily quota = 5. **B.C. and Yukon angling licences are valid on all parts of Bennett Lake, but
only one daily quota may be retained regardless of which side of the border the fish were angled, and anglers must follow tackle
restrictions and other regulations for the jurisdiction where angling**

waterbody_name: KITIMAT RIVER (Angling regulations for the Kitimat River are currently under review. Please check the in-season regulation change website prior to fishing on the Kitimat River)
raw_text:
**No Fishing** on the west half of river between fishing boundary signs near Kitimat Hatchery outfall
**No Fishing** in tributaries and upstream of Hwy 37 bridge, Mar 16-May 31
Bait ban
Hatchery rainbow trout (adipose clipped, <50 cm) daily quota = 5, no minimum size, all year
Hatchery steelhead (adipose clipped, >50 cm) daily quota = 2; province-wide hatchery steelhead annual quota = 10. When you have
caught and retained your daily steelhead quota from the Kitimat, you must stop fishing the Kitimat for the remainder of that day
No powered boats

Hatchery steelhead (adipose clipped, >50 cm) daily quota = 2; province-wide hatchery steelhead annual quota = 10. When you have caught and retained your daily steelhead quota from the Kitimat, you must stop fishing the Kitimat for the remainder of that day. No powered boats.

raw_text:
Lake trout daily quota = 3: EITHER none over 60 cm, OR only 1 over 60 cm and the other 2 must be 60 cm or less; arctic grayling daily
quota = 3 (only 1 over 35 cm); northern pike daily quota = 5 (only 1 over 70 cm); whitefish daily quota = 5. **B.C. and Yukon angling licences
are valid on all parts of Laidlaw Lake, but only one daily quota may be retained regardless of which side of the border the fish were
angled, and anglers must follow tackle restrictions and other regulations for the jurisdiction where angling**

B.C. and Yukon angling licences are valid on all parts of Laidlaw Lake, but only one daily quota may be retained regardless of which side of the border the fish were angled, and anglers must follow tackle restrictions and other regulations for the jurisdiction where angling.

quotas = 4 (only 1 over 44 cm, none between 36 cm and 44 cm); northern pike daily and possession quotas = 4 (only 1 over 100 cm, none
between 70 cm and 100 cm); whitefish daily quota = 5. **B.C. and Yukon angling licences are valid on all parts of Morley Lake, but
only one daily quota may be retained regardless of which side of the border the fish were angled, and anglers must follow tackle
restrictions and other regulations for the jurisdiction where angling**

raw_text:
**There are two separate Class II waters on the Skeena River** (non-residents and non-resident aliens require separate licences describing
which section they are fishing): **(a)** from Exchamsiks River to 1.5 km upstream of Kitsumkalum River (known as "Skeena River 2"), July 1-Sept 30; and **(b)** 1.5 km upstream of Zymoetz River (known as "Skeena River Section 4"), July 1-Dec 31; **Steelhead Stamp** not mandatory for
Skeena River 2 unless fishing for steelhead. **Steelhead Stamp** is mandatory in Skeena River Section 4 during the Classified Waters period
from July 1-Dec 31
**Angling prohibited for non-guided non-resident aliens** in Skeena River Section 4 on Saturdays and Sundays in two zones: **(a)** Shegunia
River confluence to Sedan Creek confluence July 1-Dec 31 (See map page 54), and **(b)** Chimdemash Creek confluence to 1.5 km upstream of
Zymoetz River confluence, July 1 - Dec 31, (See map page 55)
cleaned_text: There are two separate Class II waters on the Skeena River (non-residents and non-resident aliens require separate licences describing which section they are fishing): (a) from Exchamsiks River to 1.5 km upstream of Kitsumkalum River (known as 'Skeena River 2'), July 1-Sept 30; and (b) 1.5 km upstream of Zymoetz River (known as 'Skeena River Section 4'), July 1-Dec 31. Steelhead Stamp not mandatory for Skeena River 2 unless fishing for steelhead. Steelhead Stamp is mandatory in Skeena River Section 4 during the Classified Waters period from July 1-Dec 31. Angling prohibited for non-guided non-resident aliens in Skeena River Section 4 on Saturdays and Sundays in two zones: (a) Shegunia River confluence to Sedan Creek confluence July 1-Dec 31, and (b) Chimdemash Creek confluence to 1.5 km upstream of Zymoetz River confluence, July 1-Dec 31.
